generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a Windows-based Multiple Document Interface (MDI) application for real-time display of MSEL exercise events and activities.
generated_requirements.rationale: The MDI form-based architecture is a prescribed design constraint defined in TC-03 and the In Scope section.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically refresh all displayed event and activity data at a maximum interval of 60 seconds under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 1 and OC-03 specify that data age shall never exceed 60 seconds to maintain real-time situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a graphical timeline/chart view of MSEL events occupying the left two-thirds of the screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 and TC-04 mandate a fixed layout where the left two-thirds is dedicated to the graphical/chart view.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a structured data table occupying the right one-third of the screen, divided into an upper quarter for sequentially ordered events and a lower three-quarters for temporally relevant events.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02, BR-03, and TC-04 prescribe this fixed table layout to reflect how exercise controllers manage events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall color-code all displayed events and activities based on their temporal variance from current exercise time, supporting at minimum three visually distinct temporal states (e.g., overdue, active/imminent, upcoming).
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 requires color-coded temporal variance with at least three distinct states to provide immediate visual situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall simultaneously display two on-screen clocks: one showing local time and one showing current exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Future State and Gap Summary identify dual-clock support as a key capability to eliminate confusion during exercises using simulated or offset timelines.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 as read-only data sources, consuming MSEL Summary-level event and activity data without requiring modifications to those applications.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04, TC-02, and Business Objective 5 require seamless integration with both existing tools without altering them.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide direct deep-link navigation from the display interface to the corresponding record in Sync Matrix 1.0 or Exercise Management Tool 1.0 to allow in-exercise data modifications.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and the Data Entry/Modification Staff stakeholder need require that modifications be made in the originating application via a direct link from the display.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to pause the automatic display refresh, freezing all displayed data until the user explicitly resumes the refresh.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 specifies that the pause state must be user-controlled with no auto-resume, supporting coordination discussions during live exercises.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall clearly indicate on the display when the refresh is in a paused state.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 explicitly requires that the pause state be clearly indicated on the display to prevent confusion about data currency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide filtering of displayed events and activities by Venue, with the filter applied simultaneously to both the graphical view and the data table.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 and the Exercise Controller stakeholder need require consistent, simultaneous filtering across both display regions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide filtering of displayed events and activities by Key Event designation, with the filter applied simultaneously to both the graphical view and the data table.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 and the Exercise Controller stakeholder need require consistent, simultaneous filtering across both display regions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a full event list view that displays all events in the exercise regardless of current time context or active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: The In Scope section explicitly lists a full event list view as a required capability for exercise controllers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to define and configure the time segments used in the temporal display, with default time segments provided as a starting configuration.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 requires user-configurable time segments to accommodate exercises of varying duration and tempo.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate an actual-versus-assumed time chart for activities and events, comparing recorded actual execution times against planned times.
generated_requirements.rationale: The In Scope section and AAR Team stakeholder need require actual-versus-assumed time charts for post-exercise performance analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture and store a complete history report of all changes made to Activity or Event data originating from Sync Matrix 1.0, Exercise Management Tool 1.0, or the display interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 and Business Objective 7 require a full audit trail of all data changes to support after-action review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall make the history/audit report accessible to authorized users for after-action review purposes.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 and the AAR Team stakeholder need require that the history report be retrievable by authorized personnel post-exercise.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce access control on every access attempt, permitting only users who have been explicitly authorized by a system administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 and Business Objective 6 require 100% enforcement of access control with no exceptions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a system administrator interface for defining, granting, and revoking user authorization.
generated_requirements.rationale: The System Administrator stakeholder need and A-06 require an administrative capability to manage the authorized user list within the application.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display only MSEL Summary-level data in both the graphical view nodes and the data table rows; detailed drill-down data is out of scope.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and the Out of Scope section restrict the display layer to MSEL Summary-level data only.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to configure display settings including color-coding thresholds for temporal variance states.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and D-03 require that color-coding thresholds be configurable to accommodate different exercise definitions of overdue, imminent, and upcoming.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on existing client PCs without requiring new server-side infrastructure beyond what is already in place for Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 constrains deployment to existing client hardware to avoid additional infrastructure investment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 99% of the time during normal business hours (Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM–5:00 PM local time) throughout the first six months of operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 2 and OC-01 specify a 99% availability target during operational hours to ensure system readiness for live exercises.

generated_requirements.text: All planned maintenance windows shall be scheduled outside of normal business hours to minimize impact on exercise operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-01 requires that maintenance not encroach on the 99% availability window during normal business hours.

generated_requirements.text: The system's UI color scheme shall conform to the Department of Homeland Security's established color standards; any deviation shall require explicit DHS approval.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-01 and Business Objective 8 mandate DHS color schema compliance as a regulatory and branding requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system's typography, contrast ratios, and layout density shall be designed and validated to ensure legibility when projected on a 10×10 foot screen, such that 90% of viewers at 30 feet can read Event/Activity data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 and OC-02 require projection-optimized readability at standard exercise control room viewing distances.

generated_requirements.text: The system's color-coded event status indicators shall be visually distinguishable at a projection distance of 100 feet, such that 90% of viewers can determine whether events are occurring in current time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 requires that temporal status be discernible at 100 feet to support large-room group coordination.

generated_requirements.text: 90% of new users shall be able to independently initiate the display of Events or Activities within 90 minutes of first use, without formal training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 3 and UC-01 set a measurable onboarding usability target to minimize training overhead for exercise staff.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall correctly distinguish authorized from unauthorized users in 100% of access attempts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 6 and BR-06 require zero tolerance for access control failures on a DHS-affiliated system.

generated_requirements.text: Access control shall be enforced at the application level in compliance with federal information security expectations for DHS-affiliated exercise systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-02 mandates application-level access control to meet federal security compliance requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain the fixed MDI layout (left two-thirds graphical, right one-third table; table upper quarter sequential, lower three-quarters temporal) across all display configurations and screen resolutions.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-04 and BR-03 define the layout as a non-negotiable fixed constraint that must be preserved regardless of display environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Sync Matrix 1.0 (version 1.0) and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 (version 1.0) using their existing data interfaces, without requiring modifications to those applications.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 constrains integration to existing interfaces to avoid scope creep into the source applications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a visual indicator showing the timestamp of the most recent data refresh so users can assess data currency at a glance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Situational awareness requires users to know how current the displayed data is, especially when the refresh is paused.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow an authorized user to manually trigger an immediate data refresh outside of the 60-second automatic cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exercise controllers may need on-demand data updates during rapidly evolving exercise situations without waiting for the next automatic cycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display event data fields including at minimum: event name, scheduled time, venue, key event flag, and sequential/temporal classification.
generated_requirements.rationale: A-09 identifies these MSEL Summary data fields as consistently structured across both source applications and necessary for display.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall gracefully handle the unavailability of Sync Matrix 1.0 or Exercise Management Tool 1.0 by displaying a clear error or status message without crashing, and retaining the last successfully retrieved data.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-01 identifies source application unavailability as a dependency risk; graceful degradation prevents total display failure during exercises.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all access attempts, including both successful authentications and unauthorized access rejections, with timestamps.
generated_requirements.rationale: Federal security expectations (RC-02) and audit requirements (BR-10) necessitate a complete access log for security review.

generated_requirements.text: The history/audit report shall record for each change: the data field modified, the previous value, the new value, the timestamp of the change, and the source application or user responsible.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 and the AAR Team stakeholder need require sufficient detail in the audit trail to support meaningful post-exercise analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the ability to export or print the history/audit report and actual-versus-assumed time charts for use in after-action review sessions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The AAR Team stakeholder need requires accessible, shareable output from the audit and analysis features for post-exercise reporting.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to configure display settings such as default time segments and color-coding thresholds prior to exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and D-03 require pre-exercise configuration of time segments and variance thresholds by the Exercise Director or MSEL Manager.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to user interactions (e.g., filter selection, pause/resume, manual refresh) within 3 seconds under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exercise controllers operate in time-critical environments where sluggish UI responses would degrade coordination effectiveness.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase and configuration shall be structured to allow authorized IT/System Integrator personnel to update integration interfaces if source application data structures change in future versions.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-01 identifies data structure changes in source applications as a risk; maintainable integration code reduces the cost of future updates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not allow creation, editing, or deletion of MSEL event records within the EEADS interface; all such operations shall be performed exclusively in the originating source applications.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and the Out of Scope section establish EEADS as a display-only system, with the source applications as the systems of record.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support simultaneous use by multiple authorized users on separate client PCs connected to the same data sources without data conflicts or display inconsistencies.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exercise environments involve multiple controllers and observers accessing the system concurrently, requiring consistent multi-user behavior.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an on-screen help reference or quick-start guide accessible from within the application to support self-directed onboarding.
generated_requirements.rationale: UC-01 and Business Objective 3 require that 90% of users onboard without formal training, necessitating embedded guidance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate at startup that connections to Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 are available and notify the user if either source is unreachable.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-01 identifies source application availability as a critical dependency; early detection of connectivity issues prevents silent data failures during exercises.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall preserve the paused display state and all current filter and configuration settings if the application window is minimized or the display is temporarily switched, restoring the exact state upon return.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exercise controllers frequently switch between screens; state preservation prevents disruptive reconfiguration during live operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be installable and launchable on a client PC by an IT/System Integrator without requiring elevated server-side permissions beyond those already established for Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 constrains deployment to existing infrastructure, requiring a lightweight installation footprint compatible with current permissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a configuration interface allowing the system administrator to set and modify display parameters (e.g., time segment definitions, color thresholds, default filter states) without requiring code changes or redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and the System Administrator stakeholder need require runtime configurability to adapt the system to different exercises without developer intervention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall undergo projection readability validation testing on a 10×10 foot screen at 30-foot and 100-foot viewing distances prior to first live exercise deployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-02 and D-05 require formal validation of projection readability against the specified distance and screen size constraints before operational use.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall refresh the display every 60 seconds.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The application shall match the color of the schema set forth by Department of Homeland Security
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If projected the data must be readable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to read Event / Activity data from a viewing distance of 30
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be available during normal business hours. As long as the user has access to the client PC the system will be available 99% of the time during the first six months of operation.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If projected the data must be understandable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to determine that Events or Activities are occuring in current time from a viewing distance of 100
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall ensure that it can only be accessed by authorized users. The product will be able to distinguish between authorized and unauthorized users in all access attempts
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be intuitive and self-explanatory. : 90% of new users shall be able to start the display of Events or Activities within 90 minutes of using the product.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall respond fast to keep up-to-date data in the display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall have a MDI form that allows for the viewing of the graph and the data table.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display Events in a vertical table by time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display the Events in a graph by time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display Events or Activities.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The display shall have two regions: left 2/3 of the display is graphical right 1/3 of the display is a data table
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The data displayed in both the nodes within the graph and the rows in the table are MSEL Summary data
ground_truth_requirements.label: NONE

ground_truth_requirements.text: The table side of the display shall be split into 2 regions: sequential and temporal.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The top 1/4 of the table will hold events that are to occur sequentially.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The bottom 3/4 of the table will hold events that occur according to its relevance to current time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall color code events according to their variance from current time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display data from the Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 applications
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall link Events back to either the Sync Matrix 1.0 or the Exercise Managment Tool 1.0 applications for modifications.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow modification of the display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall offer a display of all the Events in the exercise.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall filter data by: Venues and Key Events.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow a user to define the time segments
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display the local and exercise time in separate clocks
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall offer the ability to pause and resume the refresh of data.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall provide charts for the Activity or Event actual versus assumed time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall provide a history report of changes made to the Activity or Event data
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require every user to authenticate with valid credentials before granting access to any application feature or data.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 mandates that only registered realtors may access the system and every session must begin with authentication.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deny access to any user who is not a registered realtor in the organization's user registry, with no provision for guest or public access.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and the regulatory constraints require strict access restriction to authorized personnel only.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to search MLS listings by specifying query criteria (e.g., location, price range, property type) and display matching results on the mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enabling field MLS search is the primary productivity objective and a core in-scope capability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display property images associated with MLS listings retrieved during a search.
generated_requirements.rationale: The scope explicitly includes display of property images as part of MLS integration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to create new MLS property listings from the mobile device and submit them to the MLS system.
generated_requirements.rationale: The future state and scope require agents to create listings directly from the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to update existing MLS property listings from the mobile device and submit changes to the MLS system.
generated_requirements.rationale: The future state and scope require agents to update listings directly from the field without relaying changes to office staff.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all data entered for MLS listing creation or update against defined MLS data quality rules before submission, and reject invalid or incomplete entries with clear, actionable error messages to the user.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 requires MLS data validation and explicit user guidance on rejection; silent acceptance is prohibited.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to save property search results to local device storage for later review, including when the device is in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 mandates that realtors can save and review search results offline.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) report based on property criteria explicitly specified by the realtor, and deliver the report within 60 seconds of criteria submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 requires realtor-specified criteria for CMA generation, and Objective 1 sets a 60-second delivery KPI.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not generate a CMA report unless the realtor has explicitly provided all required criteria inputs.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 prohibits automatic or criteria-less CMA report generation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall download and display client contact information on the mobile device, synchronized with the office back-end system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Contact management is a core in-scope capability required to eliminate the need for agents to call the office for client details.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall download and display the realtor's appointment calendar on the mobile device, synchronized with the office back-end system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Appointment management is a core in-scope capability required to eliminate scheduling inefficiencies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically synchronize contact records and appointment data with the office back-end system on a fixed 60-minute interval without requiring manual initiation by the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 mandates automatic 60-minute synchronization cadence without agent intervention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically notify the realtor when a new client appointment is recorded in the office system following a synchronization cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires proactive notification of new appointments to eliminate manual follow-up with the office.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically notify the realtor when a buyer or seller responds to an appointment request.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires prompt notification of client responses to appointment requests.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the realtor to initiate a phone call to a buyer or seller directly from within the application to schedule an appointment, only when explicitly triggered by the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 permits communication initiation only on explicit realtor action; automated outbound calls are prohibited.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a map of property locations for a specified geographic area within the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integrated mapping is an in-scope capability required to replace separate mapping applications used by agents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide turn-by-turn driving directions from the realtor's current location to a selected property, using an integrated third-party mapping service.
generated_requirements.rationale: Driving directions are an in-scope capability that eliminates the need for separate navigation tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect the absence of internet connectivity and transition to offline mode without requiring any action from the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 mandates automatic offline mode transition; offline capability is a mandatory requirement, not an enhancement.

generated_requirements.text: While in offline mode, the system shall allow the realtor to access all previously downloaded data, including MLS search results, CMA reports, contact records, and appointment information.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 specifies that previously downloaded data must remain accessible when connectivity is unavailable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent submission of any data entry that requires live MLS connectivity (e.g., creating a new listing) while the device is in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 explicitly prohibits live MLS data submissions while offline to protect data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store user preferences (including search filters, display settings, and saved results) locally on the device and preserve them across application failures, restarts, and connectivity loss.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 mandates local persistence of user preferences that survive failures and restarts.

generated_requirements.text: Upon restoration of full connectivity or application restart, the system shall restore 100% of previously saved user preferences.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 2 KPI and BR-06 require complete preference restoration after any failure or restart event.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support self-service installation by the realtor on a supported smart device without the use of printed instructions, such that 90% of untrained realtors can complete installation independently.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 4 KPI requires 90% of untrained realtors to install independently without printed documentation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be usable by realtors with no prior formal training on the application, presenting a self-explanatory interface using terminology familiar to the real estate community.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 4 and operational constraints require zero formal training sessions before productive use.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return MLS search results within 30 seconds of query submission, 100% of the time under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 1 KPI sets a hard 30-second search result response time constraint.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and deliver CMA reports within 60 seconds of the realtor submitting the required criteria.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 1 KPI sets a hard 60-second CMA report generation and delivery constraint.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain 99.9% availability, measured as 999 successful accesses out of every 1,000 attempts, on a 24-hours-per-day, 365-days-per-year basis.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 2 KPI and the operational constraints define a binding 99.9% availability SLA for continuous operation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 1,000 simultaneous users at launch without degradation of service or breach of performance SLAs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 3 KPI requires support for 1,000 concurrent users at launch to match the current registered realtor base.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall be capable of scaling to support 5,000 simultaneous users within 12 months of launch without requiring a fundamental redesign.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 3 KPI requires a scalable architecture path to 5,000 users to accommodate projected fivefold growth.

generated_requirements.text: The mobile application shall run on devices operating the Windows CE operating system and conform to the native UI standards and conventions of that platform.
generated_requirements.rationale: Technical constraints restrict the initial release to Windows CE and Palm OS platforms with native UI conformance.

generated_requirements.text: The mobile application shall run on devices operating the Palm operating system and conform to the native UI standards and conventions of that platform.
generated_requirements.rationale: Technical constraints restrict the initial release to Windows CE and Palm OS platforms with native UI conformance.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall present a consistent color scheme and typography throughout all screens, conforming to the UI standards of the target smart device platform.
generated_requirements.rationale: Design constraints require a professional, consistent visual appearance across the entire application.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the organization's existing office back-end system via its documented integration interface (API or data feed) to retrieve and submit MLS data, contact records, and appointment information.
generated_requirements.rationale: The mobile application depends entirely on the office system integration interface for all back-end data operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with a licensed third-party mapping and navigation service via a defined API to provide property maps and driving directions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mapping and navigation features depend on a third-party service per assumption A-04 and dependency D-02.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall consume the organization's existing registered realtor user list for authentication and authorization purposes, without requiring a separate user management interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Assumption A-05 specifies that the mobile system reuses the existing user registry for authentication.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce session management such that an authenticated session is required for every interaction with protected data or features, and expired or invalid sessions are terminated immediately.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 requires every session to begin with authentication, implying continuous session validity enforcement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve zero unauthorized access incidents attributable to system design deficiencies.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 5 KPI mandates zero unauthorized access incidents as a measurable security success criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically restore full online functionality, including live MLS access and synchronization, when internet connectivity is re-established after an offline period.
generated_requirements.rationale: The future state requires seamless transition between offline and online modes to maintain agent productivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall preserve all locally stored data (saved search results, CMA reports, appointments, and preferences) across application crashes and unplanned restarts.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05, BR-06, and BR-07 collectively require that locally stored data survives failures and restarts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be maintainable such that integration interface changes (e.g., back-office API versioning updates) can be applied without requiring a full application rebuild.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dependency D-01 identifies back-office API versioning changes as a risk that the architecture must accommodate.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, user-friendly error message when MLS data submission is rejected due to validation failures, specifying which fields are invalid and what corrections are required.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 requires clear user guidance on data rejection, not silent acceptance or generic error messages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the realtor with a visual or audible notification distinguishing new appointment alerts from client response alerts.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires distinct notification types for new appointments and client responses to support timely realtor action.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall clearly indicate to the realtor when the device is operating in offline mode and which features are unavailable due to lack of connectivity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Usability requires that realtors are always aware of connectivity state and feature availability to avoid confusion in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the realtor to view the details of a saved MLS property listing, including all property attributes and images, while in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 and the future state require full access to previously downloaded listing details in offline mode.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the realtor to view previously generated and saved CMA reports while in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 specifies that previously downloaded CMA reports must be accessible in offline mode.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the automatic 60-minute synchronization cycle in the background without interrupting the realtor's active use of the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 requires synchronization without manual initiation, implying it must be non-disruptive to the user experience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all authentication attempts, including failures, with sufficient detail to support security auditing and incident investigation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 5 requires zero unauthorized access incidents attributable to system design, necessitating audit trails for verification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall transmit all data between the mobile device and the back-end office system using encrypted communication channels to protect data in transit.
generated_requirements.rationale: Security constraints and the sensitivity of MLS and client data require protection of data in transit.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed such that planned maintenance activities can be scheduled and executed while remaining within the 99.9% availability SLA constraint.
generated_requirements.rationale: The operational constraint explicitly states that planned maintenance windows must be designed to fall within the 99.9% availability SLA.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The look and feel of the system shall conform to the user interface standards of the smart device.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user interface shall have standard menus buttons for navigation
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall have a professional appearance
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall have a consistent color scheme and fonts.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be used by realtors with no training.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be intuitive and self explanatory.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be easy for a realtor to learn.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall use symbols and words that are naturally understandable by the realtor community.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall produce search results in an acceptable time
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The search results shall be returned no later 30 seconds after the user has entered the search criteria
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall generate a CMA report in an acceptable time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The CMA report shall be returned no later 60 seconds after the user has entered the CMA report criteria.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall synchronize contacts and appointments in an acceptable time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall synchronize with the office system every hour.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be available for use 24 hours per day 365 days per year
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Out of 1000 accesses to the system the system is available 999 times.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall operate in offline mode whenever internet connection is unavailable.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall allow the user to view previously downloaded search results CMA reports and appointments.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall retain user preferences in the event of a failure.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 100% of saved user preferences shall be restored when system comes back online.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be able to support multiple remote users
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be able support 1000 simultaneous users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be capable of handling the existing 1000 users. This number is expected to grow 5 times within the next year.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be installed by an untrained realtor without recourse to separately-printed instructions.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 90% of untrained realtors shall be able to install the product on their device without printed instructions
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product is expected to run on Windows CE and Palm operating systems.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Only registered realtors shall be able to access the system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Every user of the system shall be authenticated and authorized.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall prevent its data from incorrect data being introduced.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow a real estate agent to query MLS information
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user shall be able to get driving directions to a selected property
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user shall be able to download appointments and contact information for clients
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display clear property images in the search results
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall update or create new property listings in the MLS
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Realtor shall be able to generate a CMA report based on property criteria
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be able to call the seller or buyer to schedule an appointment
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The realtor shall be notified of new client appointments after automatic synchronization with office system
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The realtor shall be able to retrieve a map showing property locations for a specified area
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor when a seller or buyer responds to an appointment request
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall allow the user to save the property search results.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR
